methods.description: This method involves analyzing the color and composition of sediment cores to understand post-depositional alterations and the presence of sulfides. It helps in identifying the origin of the greenish color in sediments and the role of sulfur-rich fluids.

steps.description: Collect sediment cores from different depths (375 to 740 m) and observe color variations.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment core samples and observe color variations

steps.description: Analyze the presence of reduction spots and sulfides in the cores using thermal demagnetization experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: perform thermal demagnetization experiments to detect sulfides

steps.description: Interpret the presence of pyrrhotite in green intervals as a result of fluid percolation through high porosity material.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret pyrrhotite presence due to fluid percolation

steps.description: Propose a model for sulfur-rich fluid sourcing from underlying basalts.
steps.searchable_summary: model sulfur-rich fluid sourcing from basalts

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the magnetic properties of sediment samples to understand the post-depositional chemical remanent magnetization and the effects of hydrothermal fluids.

steps.description: Collect sediment samples from green, red, and mixed color zones.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from different color zones

steps.description: Perform IRM deconvolution to determine the magnetic mineral composition.
steps.searchable_summary: perform IRM deconvolution for magnetic mineral composition

steps.description: Conduct AF demagnetization experiments to analyze the magnetic properties and remanence.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct AF demagnetization to analyze magnetic properties

steps.description: Interpret the results to understand the effects of hydrothermal fluids and post-depositional magnetization.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret magnetic data for hydrothermal fluid effects
